rulings.Case Title: J.R. MARKETING, LLC., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. HARTFORD CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Hartford Casualty Insurance Company'S Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Ethan Miller
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 3, 2011, line 4. Defendant Hartford Casualty Insurance Company's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Ethan Miller. Defendant's motion to compel deposition of Ethan Miller is denied. Defendant has failed to satisfy the Carehouse/Spectra-Physics standards. Request for sanctions is denied. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ET AL VS LAIDLAW TRANSIT INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition Answers From Environmental Law Foundation
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 3, 2011, line 1. DEFENDANT FIRST STUDENT, INC., FIRST TRANSIT, INC.'S Motion To Compel Deposition Answers From Environmental Law Foundation. Defendant's motion to compel deposition answers is granted in part. Plaintiff's witness must answer questions seeking the identity of witnesses who have knowledge of relevant information. While ELF does not have to provide a list of who was interviewed or identify on a wholesale basis the witnesses they interviewed, the identity of person who have knowledge of relevant information, whether interviewed or not by ELF, is discoverable. The motion is denied as to questions seeking information about communications between ELF and Mr. Padilla or Mr. Contreras but not the specific information these gentlemen have that is relevant to the case. The court suggests defendants depose Mr. Padilla and Mr. Contreras to find out what they know. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: EUREKA GARDEN HOMEOWNER'S ASSOCIATION VS. DONALD WILLIAM DANMEIER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 3, 2011, line 1. DEFENDANT DONALD DANMEIER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication of the 1st and 2nd cause of action is denied. Plaintiff has raised a triable issue of material fact as to whether or not plaintiffs contracted with defendant as an individual or professional corp. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication of the 5th and 6th causes of action is granted. Plaintiff does not provide any evidence that an actual controversy exists warranting declaratory relief. Alter ego liability is not a cause of action and even if it were, plaintiff has presented no admissable evidence to support this claim. =(302/LMG)
